title: Module 1
content:
Definition and examples of CPS, Integration of computing, networking, and
physical elements in CPS.
Unique vulnerabilities and attack surfaces in CPS.
CIA triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability) in CPS, Security goals
and principles tailored to CPS.
Identifying threats and assessing risks specific to CPS, Risk mitigation
strategies for CPS environments.

title: Module 2
content:
Principles of defense-in-depth in CPS, Security by design approaches for
CPS architectures.
Implementing authentication mechanisms in CPS, Role-based access
control (RBAC) and other access control models in CPS.
Symmetric and asymmetric encryption in CPS, Key management
considerations for secure communication in CPS.
Secure coding guidelines for CPS, Testing methodologies (unit testing,
integration testing) for secure CPS applications.

title: Module 3
content:
MQTT, CoAP, and other protocols used in CPS, Securing data in transit and
at rest in CPS environments.
Implementing VLANs and subnetting for network security in CPS,
Validating network segmentation strategies in CPS.
Signature-based vs. anomaly-based detection in CPS, Deploying IDPS
solutions tailored to CPS environments.
Importance of logging and monitoring for detecting security incidents in
CPS, Tools and techniques for effective monitoring in CPS.

title: Module 4
content:
Developing incident response plans (IRPs) specific to CPS, Incident
handling and escalation procedures in CPS environments.
Conducting penetration testing and vulnerability assessments in CPS,
Importance of responsible disclosure policies in CPS security.
Integrating security into the development lifecycle of CPS projects,
Continuous improvement and adaptation of CPS security practices.
Overview of cybersecurity standards applicable to CPS, Compliance
considerations in CPS deployments.
